data.title: Rhino-Rack buys assets of RockyMounts
data.description:
• Clarus (NASDAQ:CLAR) said that Rhino-Rack has acquired certain assets of RockyMounts.
  • Rhino-Rack is a brand in Clarus’s Adventure segment.
  • The terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • The deal was funded with cash on hand.

data.title: Concentra opens medical center in Knoxville
data.description:
• Concentra (NYSE:CON) said it will open a medical center in Knoxville, Tennessee.
  • The firm said it will provide work injury care, physical therapy, drug testing, DOT physical exams, pre-placement exams, and other employer-related health services from its new location in Knoxville.

data.title: Osisko Gold Royalties to commence normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Osisko Gold Royalties (NYSE:OR) said the Toronto Stock Exchange has approved its intention to commence a normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the bid, the firm may buy up to 9.33M of its common shares.

data.title: Loblaw to redeem second preferred shares, series B
data.description:
• Loblaw (TSX:L:CA) said it intends to redeem for cash all of its 9M outstanding second preferred shares, series B on January 8.
  • The company expects to redeem at a redemption price equal to C$25 apiece, for an aggregate amount of C$225 million.

data.title: Stock index futures tick lower as inflation reports loom
data.description:
Stock index futures inched lower on Monday, as investors braced for the key retail and wholesale inflation reports due later in the week.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.18%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was unchanged at 4.12%.

Key U.S. equity averages finished mixed on Friday, but the market's benchmark S&P 500 ended at a record high, with odds of a December rate cut strengthening after November's U.S. jobs report pointed to a slowing yet solid labor market.

"A further surprise for 2025 would be a hike from the Federal Reserve and events like Wednesday’s U.S. CPI will be a key determinant even if the near-term implication will be whether the Fed cut next week or not," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Looking back at last week now, risk assets put in another strong performance, with the S&P 500 rose to yet another new record, and it was a third consecutive weekly advance for the index, Reid added.

The November consumer price index will land on Wednesday, while the producer price index will be released on the day after.

The economic calendar is on the quieter side on Monday, with mainly the October wholesale inventories slated. Economists expects a rise of 0.2% M/M.

On more global news, the rule of the Assad family in Syria ended after 50 years as rebels took control of Damascus on Sunday.

data.title: Bowman Consulting selected for major 64MW solar project deal in Southeast Michigan
data.description:
• Bowman Consulting (NASDAQ:BWMN) announced that it has been selected by J. Ranck Electric, Inc. (JRE) to lead the re-mobilization of a 64 MW direct current solar project located in Southeast Michigan.
  • The project will enhance the substation’s capabilities to support additional solar power generation, integrating new photovoltaic capacity alongside existing wind power infrastructure.
  • Under this contract, Bowman will provide various services such as electrical engineering, civil design, and system modeling to help integrate JRE’s solar power installation into the US power grid.
  • The contract encompasses safety and capacity studies, including arc flash hazard assessments, short-circuit studies, and harmonics evaluations.
  • As part of its services, Bowman will also provide ongoing consulting through the construction and commissioning phases
  • This project follows a previous $1.4 million contract awarded in 2023 for engineering design services on a 200 MW solar array.

data.title: BP, Japan's JERA to merge offshore wind businesses; plan to spend up to $5.8B by 2030
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) +3.9% pre-market Monday after unveiling plans to merge its offshore wind business with that of Japanese power utility JERA to create a combined standalone company, and invest as much as $5.8B in the joint venture before the end of the decade.

BP (BP) and JERA said the 50-50 JV, to be called JERA Nex BP, will focus on the development of existing assets, initially in northwest Europe, Australia and Japan, while also pursuing new competitive projects.

The companies said JERA Nex BP will become one of the largest global offshore wind developers, owners and operators, with operating assets with ~1 GW net generating capacity, a pipeline of projects with 7.5 GW capacity, and further secured leases with 4.5 GW of potential capacity.

"This will be a very strong vehicle to grow into an electrifying world, while maintaining a capital-light model for our shareholders," BP (BP) CEO Murray Auchincloss said.

JERA, itself a 50-50 venture between Tokyo Electric Power (OTCPK:TKECF) (OTCPK:TKECY) and Chubu Electric Power (OTCPK:CHUEF), was created in 2015 to become one of the world's biggest buyers of liquefied natural gas.

data.title: Loar launches equity offering
data.description:
• Loar Holdings (NYSE:LOAR) has announced public offering of 4.75M common shares, including 1.58M shares offered by certain stockholders and rest by the company.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to 712,500 additional shares of common stock at the public offering price.
  • The net proceeds will be used for repayment of borrowings outstanding under its credit agreement and, to the extent of any remaining proceeds, for general corporate purposes, including working capital.
  • LOAR -0.7% premarket to $91.3
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Renault is seen standing out amid tough backdrop for European auto stocks
data.description:
Investors are cautious about the European automobile sector due to significant uncertainties in 2025. Key unknowns identified recently by HSBC include potential regulatory relief or CO2 fines, possible U.S. import tariffs, the impact of local US production, and tough EU-China negotiations on electric vehicles tariffs. However, the firm also thinks that despite those challenges, share prices may already reflect the anticipated headwinds.

One reason for slight optimism on the European auto sector is that experts anticipate the EU will likely offer a phased approach to CO2 regulation, as imposing large fines on automakers could negatively impact job creation and electric vehicle affordability. European automakers are also expected to focus heavily on cost-cutting measures to improve profitability, given limited prospects for revenue growth.

HSBC views Renault SA (OTCPK:RNSDF) (OTCPK:RNLSY) favorably due to its limited exposure to China and the U.S. markets. BMW (OTCPK:BMWKY) (OTCPK:BAMXF), Mercedes-Benz group AG (OTCPK:MBGAF), and Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) are also still considered attractive investments, but were highlighted as needing to demonstrate effective cost management strategies. Meanwhile, the firm thinks Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VLKAF) could surprise positively in 2025, given low market expectations and ongoing self-improvement efforts.

HSBC is more cautious on Stellantis (STLA), with the auto giant seen needing to prove its issues are temporary rather than structural.

Overall, the European auto market is forecast to grow 4.0% to 4.5% in 2025, despite the various challenges. The outlook for electric vehicle market share has worsened, with S&P Global revising its forecast for BEV market share down to 21% for 2025 from 27%.

data.title: Interpublic stock gains on deal to be acquired by peer Omnicom
data.description:
Omnicom (NYSE:OMC) to acquire Interpublic (NYSE:IPG) in a stock-for-stock transaction, sending the peer advertising company's shares rallying +15% in premarket trade.

Under the terms of the agreement, Interpublic (IPG) shareholders will receive 0.344 Omnicom (OMC) shares for each share of Interpublic common stock they own.

Following the close of the transaction, Omnicom (OMC) shareholders will own 60.6% of the combined company and Interpublic shareholders will own 39.4%, on a fully diluted basis. The transaction is expected to generate annual cost synergies of $750 million.

"This combination represents a tremendous strategic opportunity for our stakeholders, amplifying our investments in platform capabilities and talent as part of a more expansive network," said Philippe Krakowsky, Interpublic's CEO said.

John Wren will remain Chairman & CEO of Omnicom. Phil Angelastro will remain EVP & CFO of Omnicom.

Philippe Krakowsky and Daryl Simm will serve as Co-Presidents and COOs of Omnicom, while Krakowsky will also be Co-Chair of the Integration Committee post-merger.

The combined company will retain the Omnicom name and trade under the OMC ticker symbol on the New York Stock Exchange.

IPG +14.80% premarket to $33.59, Omnicom (OMC) down -1.54% to $101.83.

The all-stock agreement is likely to value Interpublic (IPG) in a range of $13 billion to $14 billion excluding debt, the Journal reported. Interpublic’s (IPG) market value was about $11 billion as of Friday.

Source: Press Release
